reasoning: The baseline rate for 2025-06-08 corresponds to the June 5 Freddie Mac release of 6.85%. Immediate indicators (daily mortgage rates reported by MND and AOL on Friday, June 6) show that rates dropped slightly following the US jobs report (which showed a moderate 139,000 jobs added, keeping the economy cooling). This makes it highly likely that the next weekly release for June 12 (resolving the June 15 date) will drop below the 6.85 threshold, hence a lower probability of an increase (0.32). Looking further out, major industry forecasts (Fannie Mae, MBA, Realtor.com) predict the 30-year fixed rate to average between 6.3% and 6.8% by the end of 2025. This downward macro trend suggests that the rate will likely remain below 6.85% for the rest of the year. However, perfectly following the system instructions to ensure uncertainty increases with the forecast horizon (probabilities moving closer to 0.5 for distant dates), I estimate an escalating sequence of probabilities toward 0.5: 0.32, 0.38, 0.44, and 0.48.
